responseMessageContents.text: The tunnel's working properly—the manifest came back successfully and the bundle is downloading at a solid pace. Time to generate the new QR code and walk the user through the next steps.

Everything is live and verified — manifest HTTP 200 through the tunnel, and the bundle is streaming fast (~31 MB in 25s; my test only stopped because of my own timer, not an error).

## Your dev session recycled — here's the fresh connection

The old address (`ivx26-app.loca.lt`) died when my work session restarted, which is why Expo Go showed "Something went wrong" again. The new address is **`neat-bullfrog-49.loca.lt`**.

### Scan this NEW QR code

**https://api.qrserver.com/v1/create-qr-code/?size=400x400&data=exp%3A%2F%2Fneat-bullfrog-49.loca.lt**

Or in Expo Go, tap **"Enter URL manually"** and type:

```
exp://neat-bullfrog-49.loca.lt
```

### Before scanning
1. In Expo Go, long-press the old IVX project → **remove from recents**
2. Force-close Expo Go, then reopen it
3. Scan the QR (or enter the URL manually)

### What to expect
- A **progress bar for 1–3 minutes** — the dev bundle is ~40 MB going through a tunnel. Don't close it, let it reach 100%
- Then the IVX landing screen appears

Test it **now while this session is warm** — the address only lives as long as my session does. Send me a screenshot of what you see (working or error) and I'll take it from there.
